steps.title: Identify the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage opens with what archaeologists assume—that large-scale ancient engineering required an elite directing class. The word “However” then signals a pivot to a recent excavation that complicates this assumption. The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It describes a common view among archaeologists, then discusses a recent finding that challenges that view.': Matches exactly: the assumption (common view) followed by the canal excavation (recent challenging finding). ✓
  • Choice B — 'It outlines a method used in some archaeological fieldwork, then explains why an alternative method is superior to it.': No method comparison is made; the passage does not argue one approach is superior. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It presents contradictory conclusions drawn by archaeologists, then evaluates a study that has apparently resolved that contradiction.': There is one assumption, not two contradictory conclusions, and nothing is presented as resolved. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It identifies a gap in scientific research, then presents a strategy used by some archaeologists to remedy that gap.': The passage identifies an assumption, not a research gap, and proposes no remedial strategy. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
